stemHtml:
Which statements correctly describe operational considerations of steel brakes ?

1. Brake wear is primarily a function of the energy absorbed during braking 
2. Brake wear depends mainly on the number of brake applications 
3. It is generally preferred to minimise use of the brake applications during taxi 
4. Steel brakes can absorb less heat energy than carbon brakes. 
5. Steel brakes have a higher friction coefficient than carbon brakes
